title: Week 10 PPTX: Software Development
summary: Source slide deck for Week 10 covering software development, project workflow, full-stack structure, and development process diagrams.
content_html:

Source file: w10-SwDev-Major.pptx · Type: PPTX slide deck · Visual pages kept: 29 · Intro/admin pages filtered: 2

Slide 5
(D) CRUD applications
5

Slide 6
App: CRUD (Album Manager)
URL: Embedding SQL
Code set: in the Exercise File
Language: PHP
DBMS: SQLite
Deploy and Demo: on XAMPP
6

Slide 7
App: CRUD (Album Manager)
7

Slide 8
App: SQL Interactive Demonstrator (SID)
URL: Embedding SQL
Code set: in the Exercise File of the course
Language: PHP
DBMS: SQLite
Deploy and Demo: on XAMPP
8

Slide 9
Set up the app
Install XAMPP to a folder, denote by $XAMPP:
e.g. $XAMPP = c:\Program files\xampp
Start the Apache web server
Copy the app source code folder (e.g. CRUD), from the Exercise folder to $XAMPP/htdocs
Access the app on the browser:
e.g. http://localhost/CRUD/crud.php
9

Slide 10
How do you use MySQL?
From XAMPP, start the MySQL server
Read the header of the the main application script
crud.php
sid.php
Create the MySQL user account and database specified in the header (e.g. album)
Populate the database with initial data:
use the corresponding SQL script provided in the SQL subfolder
Access the app on the browser:
e.g. http://localhost/CRUD/crud.php
10

Slide 11
Analysis: Use Case Diagram
Model the behaviours of a system:
business system
computing system
Use case is either:
a business function OR
a system function
provided for an interaction by an actor
11
https://www.uml-diagrams.org/use-case-diagrams.html

Slide 12
Example: Album Manager
Actor: (Sales) staff
System functions:
view album list
add, edit, delete an album
Other functions:
what are they?
12
https://www.uml-diagrams.org/use-case-diagrams.html

Slide 13
Analysis/Design:
Class Diagram
Model the system structure
analysis & design
Elements:
class
interface
property (attribute)
operation
association
constraint
13
https://www.uml-diagrams.org/class-diagrams-overview.html

Slide 14
Example: Class Diagram
14

Slide 15
Design: Sequence Diagram
Describes object interactions in detail
Key elements:
object lifelines
messages
15
https://www.uml-diagrams.org/sequence-diagrams.html

Slide 16
Domain Driven Software Development
Domain Driven Design (Eric Evans, 2003)
Domain model is the core (“heart”) of the software
the business logic
designed with domain experts
implementation feasible
Software (UI, database, etc.) is designed around the domain model
16

Slide 17
JDA Software framework
JDA: Java-based software framework
https://github.com/jdomainapp/jda
Features:
Domain Driven Design (DDD)
Java (>= 8)
Frontend:
web frameworks (Vuejs, React, Angular)
Mobile: ReactNative
Backend: SpringBoot (Nodejs: todo)
Automatic software generation from domain model
Maven: multi-project configuration
core, common, modules
Open source
On-going student projects

Slide 18
Example: CourseMan
Domain model
18

Slide 19
Example: Software design (1)
Conceptual & logical
19

Slide 20
Example: Software design (2) Physical
20
@ModuleDescriptor(name = "ModuleStudent",
modelDesc = @ModelDesc(model = Student.class),
viewDesc = @ViewDesc(
formTitle = "Form: Student",
imageIcon = "Student.png",
domainClassLabel = "Student",
viewType= RegionType.Data,
parent= RegionName.Tools,
view = View.class),
controllerDesc = @ControllerDesc(
controller= Controller.class,
isDataFieldStateListener = true)
,isPrimary=true
,setUpDesc = @SetUpDesc(postSetUp = CopyResourceFilesCommand.class)
)
class ModuleStudent { ... }

Slide 21
Example: Software design (3) Physical
21
@ModuleDescriptor(...)
public class ModuleStudent {
@AttributeDesc(label = "Manage Students")
private String title;
@AttributeDesc(label = "Student ID")
private int id;
@AttributeDesc(label = "Full Name")
private String name;
...
@AttributeDesc(label = "Current Address",
type = JComboField.class,
ref=@Select(clazz= Address.class,
attributes={"name"}),
loadOidWithBoundValue=true,
isStateEventSource=true)
private Address address;
}

Slide 22
Example: Software design (4) Physical
22
@ModuleDescriptor(...)
public class ModuleStudent {
@AttributeDesc(label = "Manage Students")
private String title;
...
@AttributeDesc(label = "Course Enrolments",
type = JObjectTable.class
)
private Collection<Enrolment> enrolments;
}

Slide 23
Example: Software design (5) Physical
23
@ModuleDescriptor(name = "ModuleSClass",
...
controllerDesc = @ControllerDesc(
controller = Controller.class,
// open with all objects and open children
openPolicy = OpenPolicy.O_C
))
public class ModuleStudentClass {
...
@AttributeDesc(label = "Students",
type = DefaultPanel.class,
controllerDesc = @ControllerDesc(
// default: openPolicy=OpenPolicy.I
// load all student objects and
// those of the descendant modules
openPolicy = OpenPolicy.L_C
))
private Collection<Student> students;
}
